reference_review:
POINTS:
- [transports/http/deployment/src/test/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/test/sampling/SamplingTest.java:158] So if I understand it correctly this PR does not try to solve the "tool execution" part, i.e. it's up to the MCP tool author to process the `tool_use` request from the server. I.e. the MCP tool author would need to create the `ToolResultContent` manually, in a subsequent/nested sampling request.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/Content.java:11] This does not reflect the `ContentBlock` schema https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/schema#contentblock. And I think that we should try to keep the types in sync.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/SamplingMessage.java:14] I think that we will need to introduce an analogy for [`SamplingMessageContentBlock`](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/schema#samplingmessagecontentblock).

Also we should try to keep the backward compatibility. So I think that we should change the canonical constructor to accept a list of `SamplingContent` (see my comment above).  This change is not fully source-compatible if anyone uses record patterns, regular construction via the convenience constructor is fine.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/ToolChoice.java:18] Maybe add some javadoc?

POINTS:
- [independent-projects/arc/processor/src/main/java/io/quarkus/arc/processor/BeanDeployment.java:193] I wonder if we should detect annotation-valued members as well...

Also I think that for annotations it's more common to use the term `member` instead of `method`.
- [independent-projects/arc/processor/src/main/java/io/quarkus/arc/processor/BeanDeployment.java:867] Would it make sense to resuse the logic from above?
- [extensions/arc/deployment/src/test/java/io/quarkus/arc/test/stereotype/DoubleScopedStereotypeTestCase.java:38] This apparently breaks the `DoubleScopedStereotypeTestCase` :thinking:

PR DESCRIPTION:
This PR is a port of the existing independent [JSR305CheckstylePlugin](https://github.com/bjrke/JSR305CheckstylePlugin) into the sevntu ecosystem.

Documentation and discussion can be found in issue #734.

+Jsr305AnnotationsCheck.desc=Checks method parameters and return values for the presence of @Nonnull, @Nullable, or @CheckForNull annotations.
+Jsr305AnnotationsCheck.packages=Sets the parent package for all classes that will be checked.
+Jsr305AnnotationsCheck.excludePackages=Packages excluded from checking. This can be useful if under the parent package set with "packages" there are subpackages which should not be checked.

input:
REPO: sevntu-checkstyle/sevntu.checkstyle PR #709: Issue #464: Check for trailing commas on enums Changed files (11): eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.properties, eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.xml, sevntu-checks/sevntu-checks.xml, sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java, sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/NumericLiteralNeedsUnderscoreCheck.java, sevntu-checks/src/main/resources/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/messages.properties, sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheckTest.java, sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/internal/CheckstyleRegressionTest.java, sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/internal/CommitValidationTest.java, sevntu-checks/src/test/resources/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputEnumTrailingCommaCheck.java, sevntu-checkstyle-sonar-plugin/src/main/resources/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/sonar/checkstyle-extensions.xml Diff (+317/-4): PR DESCRIPTION: Addresses #464, supersedes #465 and #474. This Check assures that the last enum constant definition in a multi-line enum ends in a comma: ``` enum E1 { ONE, TWO, THREE, } ``` Usage examples are in the Javadoc of `EnumTrailingCommaCheck`. This check follows the concept of [ArrayTrailingComma](http://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/config_coding.html#ArrayTrailingComma), hence the similar name. It also checks that the "trailing" comma after the last enum constant definition is not followed by a semicolon, because this would miss the point of the check: clean diff and easy manipulation. DIFF: --- eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.properties (+3/-0) --- @@ -183,6 +183,9 @@ EitherLogOrThrowCheck.loggingMethodNames = Logging method names separated with c WhitespaceBeforeArrayInitializerCheck.name = Whitespace Before Array Initializer WhitespaceBeforeArrayInitializerCheck.desc = This checks enforces whitespace before array initializer. +EnumTrailingCommaCheck.name = Enum Trailing Comma +EnumTrailingCommaCheck.desc = This checks enforces a trailing comma at the end of a line in an enum constant definition. + MoveVariableInsideIfCheck.name = Move Variable Inside If Check MoveVariableInsideIfCheck.desc = Checks if a variable is only used inside if statements and asks for it's declaration to be moved there too. --- eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.xml (+7/-0) --- @@ -556,5 +556,12 @@ + + + %EnumTrailingCommaCheck.desc + + + + --- sevntu-checks/sevntu-checks.xml (+1/-0) --- @@ -157,6 +157,7 @@ + --- sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java (+142/-0) --- @@ -0,0 +1,142 @@ +//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// +// checkstyle: Checks Java source code for adherence to a set of rules. +// Copyright (C) 2001-2018 the original author or authors. +// +// This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or +// modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public +// License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either +// version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version. +// +// This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, +// but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of +// MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU +// Lesser General Public License for more details. +// +// You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public +// License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software +// Foundation, Inc., 59 Temple Place, Suite 330, Boston, MA 02111-1307 USA +//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// + +package com.github.sevntu.checkstyle.checks.coding; + +import com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.api.AbstractCheck; +import com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.api.DetailAST; +import com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.api.TokenTypes; + +/** + * Checks if enum constant contains an optional trailing comma. + * + *

Rationale: Putting this comma in makes is easier to change the order of the elements or add + * new elements at the end of the list. This is similar to + * ArrayTrailingComma. + * + *

The following is a normal enum type declaration: + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA
+ * }
+ *
+ * + *

However, if you want to append something to the list, you would need to change the line + * containing the last enum constant: + * + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA,   // changed due to the ','
+ *     DELTA    // new line
+ * }
+ *
+ * + *

This check makes sure that also the last enum constant has a trailing comma, which is + * valid according to the Java Spec (see Enum Constants) + * + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA,
+ *     DELTA, // removing this comma will result in a violation with the check activated
+ * }
+ *
+ * + *

However, you could also add a semicolon behind that comma on the same line, which would raise + * a violation + * + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA,
+ *     DELTA,; // violation
+ * }
+ *
+ * In this case the semicolon should be removed. However, if there is more in the enum body, the + * semicolon should be placed on a line by itself. + * + *

An example of how to configure the check is: + * 

+ * <module name="EnumTrailingComma"/>
+ *
+ * + *

Please note

		if ( jdbcTypeCode == ARRAY ) {
			int startIndex = columnTypeName.indexOf( '<' ) + 1;

			if ( endIndex > startIndex ) {
				final String componentTypeName = columnTypeName.substring( startIndex, endIndex ).trim();

- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/jpa/query/CriteriaUpdateWithParametersTest.java:81] It's a bit odd that Spanner can't infer the type of the parameter based on the assignment target, but anyway, you can fix this by overriding `visitSetAssignment` in your `SqlAstTranslator` with:

```java
	@Override
	protected void visitSetAssignment(Assignment assignment) {
		withParameterRenderingMode( SqlAstNodeRenderingMode.NO_PLAIN_PARAMETER, () -> super.visitSetAssignment( assignment ) );
	}
```
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/query/ManyToManyGroupByOrderByTest.java:115] Huh, so even though the same expression is used in the select clause, it complains about this... Sounds like you might be having a bug or missing feature ;)
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/annotations/Discount.java:23] Hmm, this statement doesn't make a lot of sense to me. AFAICS, we should render something like:

```sql
update Discount d1_0 
set d1_0.discount=?,
d1_0.CUSTOMER_ID=?
where d1_0.id=?
```

in which case there would be no ambiguity anymore. Am I missing something?
- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/jpa/CriteriaUpdateAndDeleteWithJoinTest.java:58] Hmm, but it shouldn't render the parameter since you have overridden `SpannerDialect#castType`. My understanding is that it will call `org.hibernate.type.descriptor.sql.internal.DdlTypeImpl#getCastTypeName`, but the result should be just `string`. Can you please dig a bit into this so we can understand what is going on?
